slides.headline: Standing Beside Those Who Answer the Call
slides.subheadline: For over seven decades, The 200 Club of Monmouth County has supported first responders and their families — with immediate aid, scholarships, and recognition for extraordinary bravery.

slides.headline: Over $100,000 Awarded in 2025
slides.subheadline: Each year we invest in the children of Monmouth County first responders. Applications are now open for the Annual and Ben Danskin Scholarships.

slides.headline: Honoring Bravery at the Annual Valor Awards
slides.subheadline: We recognize first responders who go above and beyond. Nominations are now being accepted for the 54th Annual Awards Luncheon on June 5.

missionBar.text: When tragedy strikes a first responder's family, we respond within hours — not weeks.

pillars.title: Three Pillars. One Commitment.
paragraphs: For over seven decades, The 200 Club has stood beside first responders and their families with immediate, unconditional support. Our work centers on three pillars: family assistance in moments of crisis, scholarships for the next generation, and recognition of extraordinary bravery.

items.title: Family Assistance
items.description: When a Monmouth County first responder is killed or seriously injured in the line of duty, we respond within hours with financial support for their family — no paperwork, no red tape.

items.title: College Scholarships
items.description: Each year we award scholarships to children of Monmouth County first responders. Over $3 million awarded to date — and over $100,000 in 2025 alone.

items.title: Valor Awards
items.description: We honor first responders who go above and beyond — from Certificates of Recognition to our highest honor, the Valor Award.

spotlight.title: When a gun hits the street, someone has to get it off.
spotlight.description: Officer Dominique Russo, five-foot-one, saw a cocked .45 in clear sight. She chased the suspect on foot, took him to the ground single-handedly, and got the weapon off the street. "I'm just doing what I signed up for," she said later.
spotlight.attributionDetail: Neptune Township Police Department · Valor Award Recipient

scholarships.title: Investing in the Next Generation
paragraphs: Each year, The 200 Club awards college scholarships to children of first responders in Monmouth County. In 2025, we awarded over $100,000 in scholarships — and to date, more than $3 million has gone to the families of those who serve.
The Ben Danskin Scholarship is also awarded annually to one exceptional junior or senior who demonstrates outstanding merit.

stat.label: Awarded in 2025 to children of Monmouth County first responders.

awards.title: Annual Scholarship
awards.description: Open to undergraduate children of active 200 Club member first responders in Monmouth County.

awards.title: Ben Danskin Scholarship
awards.description: A merit-based award for one exceptional junior or senior demonstrating outstanding achievement.

valor.title: Recognizing Extraordinary Bravery
paragraphs: Each year at our Annual Awards Luncheon, The 200 Club recognizes first responders who went above and beyond. Awards range from Certificates of Recognition to The Valor Award — our highest honor.

awards.title: The Valor Award
awards.description: Highest honor — extraordinary bravery that placed an officer's life at risk.

awards.title: Meritorious Service Award
awards.description: Exceptional performance of duty demonstrating outstanding skill and judgment.

awards.title: Certificate of Recognition
awards.description: Acknowledges first responders for notable acts of service to the community.

events.title: Join Us This Year
events.description: From our Annual Awards Luncheon to golf outings and scholarship socials — every event supports our mission.

items.title: 54th Annual Awards Luncheon & Valor Awards
items.description: Our signature event honoring Monmouth County first responders for extraordinary bravery. Open to members and the public.

items.title: 15th Annual Scholarship Social
items.description: Celebrating our scholarship recipients and the families of first responders who make it all possible.

items.title: 43rd Annual Golf Outing
items.description: Our longstanding golf outing at Deal Golf & Country Club — a day on the course in support of first responders and their families.

join.title: Two Ways to Stand With First Responders
join.description: Become a member to join a community that has backed Monmouth County first responders since 1971, or make a one-time donation to directly support our mission.

membership.title: Become a Member
membership.description: Members are the backbone of The 200 Club. Your annual dues directly fund family assistance when tragedy strikes a first responder.
benefits: Supports immediate aid to families of fallen or injured first responders
Enables scholarships for first responders' children
Your dependents are eligible for annual scholarships
membership.fine: Membership is subject to approval by the Executive Board. Must be referred by an existing member.

donate.title: Make a Donation
donate.description: Your gift helps us deliver financial support to families in crisis and fund life-changing scholarships for first responders' children.
benefits: Immediate aid to families of fallen or injured first responders
Supports college scholarships for children of first responders
Funds Valor Awards and community programs
100% of donations go to mission programs
donate.qrNote: Scan with your phone camera to open PayPal directly.
donate.fine: Donations are separate from membership dues and go directly to our mission programs.

caseStudies.title: Real People. Real Bravery.
paragraphs: These are a few of the first responders recognized by The 200 Club in recent years — and the moments that defined their service.

items.title: Officer Dominique Russo
items.story: Five-foot-one. A cocked .45 in clear sight. Officer Russo chased the suspect on foot, took him to the ground single-handedly, and got the gun off the street. "I'm just doing what I signed up for."

items.title: Five Marlboro Officers
items.story: A teenager pinned between two cars — one on fire. Officers used a patrol vehicle to pry the cars apart and pulled 17-year-old Rebecca Kniffin to safety. They were later reunited at the Valor Awards ceremony.

items.title: Officer Adam Sosnowski
items.story: A suicidal man drew a weapon and pointed it directly at him. Officer Sosnowski disarmed him through quick action and training — choosing to save a life rather than take one.
